example.note: Ko after bahay, just like with family members.

cards.body: Tahanan means 'home.' Not the building, but the feeling. Where your pamilya is.

cards.source_text: What's the difference between bahay and tahanan?
options: Bahay = big house, tahanan = small house
cards.explanation: Bahay is the physical building. Tahanan is the emotional concept of home.

example.note: Ko after pamilya. Same pattern as everything else.

alam_mo_ba_inline.fact: Filipino families are among the closest in the world. Multi-generational households are normal. Lola, Tita, and cousins often live under one roof.

cards.explanation: Pamilya ko = my family. Ko = my.
Tahanan ko dito = my home is here.
Bahay ko, bahay mo = My house is your house. Classic Filipino hospitality.

wrong_answer_tracking.behaviour: Wrong answers go to back of current session queue.

alam_mo_ba: 'Bahay ko, bahay mo' (my house is your house) isn't just a saying. Filipino homes genuinely operate on open-door hospitality. Guests are always fed.
Multi-generational living is the norm, not the exception. Many Filipino families have 3-4 generations under one roof.
Pamilya from Spanish 'familia.' Bahay is pure Tagalog. Tahanan is pure Tagalog. The mix tells you which concepts came from where.
